name: VESICOURETERIC REFLUX
definition: abnormal valve mechanism at UVJ resulting in reflux, ureteral dilatation, clubbed calices,

demographics.Location: uni- / bilateral (frequently involves lower pole ureter in total ureteral

distribution: uni- / bilateral (frequently involves lower pole ureter in total ureteral
discriminating_features: intermittent hydroureteronephrosis = variable size of collecting system
redundant mucosa causing apparent thickening of renal pelvic wall
midline-to-orifice distance > 7-9 mm has high probability of vesicoureteric reflux

general: intermittent hydroureteronephrosis = variable size of collecting system
redundant mucosa causing apparent thickening of renal pelvic wall
midline-to-orifice distance > 7-9 mm has high probability of vesicoureteric reflux

clinical.Cx: reflux atrophy / nephropathy in 22-50%; end-stage renal disease in 5-15% of adults
